L1.area: Container execution and filesystem mount lifecycle management

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the container build process where the cleanup logic that removes temporary files created for mountpoints can be tricked into deleting files outside the container's root directory on the host system. A malicious build frontend or build definition can exploit this to achieve arbitrary host file deletion.

L3.area: Container execution and filesystem mount lifecycle management
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the container build process where the cleanup logic that removes temporary files created for mountpoints can be tricked into deleting files outside the container's root directory on the host system. A malicious build frontend or build definition can exploit this to achieve arbitrary host file deletion.

ground_truth.reason: The mount stub cleanup routine does not validate that resolved paths remain within the container root, allowing a malicious build frontend to craft symlinks that cause the cleaner to delete arbitrary files on the host filesystem.
